summary: Myasthenia gravis is an autoimmune disorder characterized by autoantibody-mediated destruction or blockade of acetylcholine receptors at the neuromuscular junction, leading to impaired neurotransmission and clinically evident fatigue-related muscular weakness. The central pathophysiologic process involves direct dysfunction at the neuromuscular junction, confirmed by both symptom patterns and therapeutic interventions targeting this specific interface.
